markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helmsman's Recourse](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1858)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Meteor Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [standard-grade duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2815) [meteor shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13) (Hardness 7, HP 28, BT 14) is a wheel from a ship. While wielding the shield, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You're in a body of water; **Effect** For 10 minutes, you don't sink if you haven't succeeded at a Swim action on a turn, and if you're submerged, you automatically ascend 10 feet at the end of your turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 60, and BT 30, and when you Activate the shield, it casts [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) on you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 2,650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 11, HP 80, and BT 40; the item bonus is +2; and when you Activate the shield, you can breathe underwater for 10 minutes.
name: Helmsman's Recourse

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sanguine Klar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1859)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Klar](/Shields.aspx?ID=12)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [klar](/Shields.aspx?ID=12)_ (Hardness 8, HP 64, BT 32) is built with spikes made of beasts' fangs.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** You or a creature within 5 feet of you suffers [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage;  **Effect** The _sanguine klar_ regains a number of Hit Points equal to the bleed damage, up to an amount equal to its Hardness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Klar
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Klar (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 3,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

A _greater sanguine klar_ (Hardness 13, HP 100, and BT 50) is a _+2 greater striking wounding klar_, and its activation can be triggered by bleed damage at a range of 30 feet.
name: Sanguine Klar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sapling Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1860)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=1), [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This surprisingly weighty, though well-balanced, wooden [buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=1) is emblazoned with the image of a sapling. The sapling withers as the shield takes damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The buckler expands, with the sapling image growing into a mighty oak tree. The buckler becomes a [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4), gaining the corresponding AC bonus, Speed penalty, and ability to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90). It keeps the same Hit Points and Broken Threshold, but its Hardness and Bulk increase by 2 in this form. The shield remains in this form until you Activate it again to revert it to a buckler.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"

pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

The buckler has Hardness 3, HP 24, and BT 12.

[Starfall Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1861)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 23,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Heavy Rondache](/Shields.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [high-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=310) [heavy rondache](/Shields.aspx?ID=10) (Hardness 17, HP  100, BT 50) bears a _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41)_ and has dents that resemble craters on its surface. While wielding the shield, you have fire resistance 15.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You Stride up to three times. This movement doesn't trigger reactions and ignores difficult terrain. At the end of your movement, you deal 6d10 bludgeoning damage in a 10-foot emanation and 14d6 fire damage in a 40-foot emanation (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save, with the results applying to both the bludgeoning and fire damage). The space where you end your movement and all adjacent spaces become difficult terrain for 1 minute, and the shield glows red-hot for 10 minutes, during which it gains the effect of the [_greater flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Starfall Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warding Escutcheon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1863)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 23

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An image of a keyhole adorns the coat of arms of this stone [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) (Hardness 10, HP 40, BT 20).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield becomes a locked door in an empty, adjacent space no wider than 5 feet, becoming flush with the surrounding structure so it blocks all passage. The door has the same Hardness, HP, and Broken Threshold as the shield, with [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) DCs of 25 to open. The door remains in place for 1 hour, until it's opened or broken, or until you spend an action to end this effect, at which point the shield returns to your hand (or falls to the ground in your space if your hands are full).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield floats lazily in the air as bricks quickly fly from it to form a squat stone tower around it. You cast [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=681), with the shield becoming the door.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Authorized](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1864)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 32

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp needles impale anyone who attempts to wield this weapon other than its rightful owner. Each _authorized_ rune is etched with the blood of a specific creature. If any other creature wields the weapon, needles immediately erupt from the weapon's hilt or shaft, dealing 1d8 piercing damage plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the wielder. If the weapon has a _[striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ rune, this damage increases to 1d8 per damage die and 1d4 persistent damage per damage die; this counts only the weapon's base die and dice from the _striking_ rune. The persistent bleed damage can't end while the creature still holds the weapon. The spikes retract once the creature lets go.

 When the rune is crafted, the crafter can choose to broaden the criteria for who can safely wield the item, expanding the users to creatures with a particular bloodline, patron deity, or sanctification, as the crafter chooses. This criterion must be shared by the creature who contributed the blood for the rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Authorized

    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"

[Coating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1865)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 32

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When etched, this rune creates an extradimensional space that links to the weapon that wields it. The space can hold up to 1 Bulk but can contain only [poisons](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9) and [magic oils](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=17) that could be applied to the weapon. Stowing or retrieving an item in the space requires an Interact action, except when using the rune's activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** At least one magic oil or poison is stored inside the rune's extradimensional space; **Effect** For 1 minute, you can apply stored oils and poisons to the weapon without needing any hands free. Applying them takes the same number of actions as normal. An oil or poison applied this way pours directly from the extradimensional space onto the weapon, and when it's fully applied, its empty vial is ejected.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coating

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune makes a weapon capable of impossible offense and defense. The etched weapon is immune to [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) and similar effects that could [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) its magic. If it's a ranged weapon or [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon, its range increment is doubled.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You and the weapon flash to a perfect attacking position, then return to where you started. Make a Strike with the etched weapon against one creature you can see, even if the target is beyond the weapon's reach or range. On this Strike, ignore any circumstance penalty, status penalty, and range increment penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Merciful_ weapons are sheathed in an unmistakable wispy green aura recognized by both gladiators and guards around the world. A merciful weapon has the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait and can't be used to make a lethal attack. Any [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) the weapon would deal is negated.

 On a critical hit, a _merciful_ weapon causes the target to become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the weapon's wielder for 1 minute, as the target is bombarded with feelings of guilt and remorse. This is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect. If you have access to the weapon's [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235), you choose whether to use it or the merciful effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Merciful

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 35 **Price** 4,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47) --- The [Empyreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=16) word for “repent” is etched in golden lettering on the shaft of this _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [merciful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1869) [dawnsilver ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_. You cast [_protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=238) targeting yourself. **Redeemer Wielder** If you're a [champion](/Classes.aspx?ID=4) of the [redemption](/Causes.aspx?ID=14) cause, you also gain the following two benefits.
  • When you critically hit an unholy creature with the _blessed reformer_, until the end of your next turn, the target takes a –10-foot penalty to its Speeds and can't Step.
  • You can Activate the weapon in the following way.
**Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You hit a creature using the _blessed reformer_ as your last action; **Effect** You cast [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) at the same level as your champion focus spells. It must target the creature you hit, and the creature takes a status penalty on its save against the spell equal to the _blessed reformer's_ number of weapon damage dice. **Craft Requirements** You're a champion with the redemption cause. The initial materials must include 140 gp of [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917).

---

The blade of this alabaster _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9)_ grows thick and pitted as the moon waxes, and it thins to a sharp sliver as the moon wanes. Moonlight also causes a second rune on the blade to change shape with the moon's phases: during the new and crescent moon, the weapon has the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune; during the quarter moon, the [_fearsome_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=731) rune; and during the gibbous and full moon, the [_wounding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) rune.

 When wielded under moonlight of any strength, the reaper_'s crescent_ deals additional cold damage equal to the number of damage dice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reaper's Crescent

>
[Stargazer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1880)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 35

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

</row>

</row>

---

When you invest this [_clear quartz crystal ball_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=253), it orbits your head like an [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). As long as you have the _stargazer_ invested, you can use an Interact action to direct it to orbit one of your hands where you can telekinetically smash the orb into foes, wielding it as a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2)_. While you're directing the _stargazer_, your hand is full, and you can send it back to your head with another Interact action. On a critical hit, the _stargazer_ pulses with hypnotic starlight, [dazzling](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) the struck creature for 1 round. A stargazer doesn't add [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

</column>

>
[Alicorn Lance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1882)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 36

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The version of the alicorn lance which does void damage may not be purchased or crafted in Pathfinder Society play._

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This white _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=28)_ is made from the horn of a [unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397), willingly granted at the end of its lifetime. In addition to its normal damage, it deals 1 spirit damage on successful attacks. You can make the lance glow like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or suppress its light by using an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You evoke the spirit of the unicorn that donated the _alicorn lance's_ horn, which you ride in a shining charge. Move up to twice your Speed and make a Strike with the _alicorn lance_; you gain the effects of the lance's [jousting](/Traits.aspx?ID=186) trait on this Strike. If you were already mounted when you Activate the horn, the unicorn spirit takes shape around your steed, granting it a +10–foot status bonus to its Speed for the charge.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a horn willingly gifted by a unicorn. In rare instances, an _alicorn lance_ can be made with a forcibly taken horn. However this heinous act pollutes the horn's magic, causing it to glow with a sickly red light; remove the holy trait, add the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait to Strikes instead of the holy trait, and make the unicorn spirit evoked in the charge whinny visibly in pain.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alicorn Lance

This _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_ is covered in scales shed by or harvested from a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395). When benefiting from the circumstance bonus to AC granted by the bo staff’s [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait, you also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) effects of the tradition matching the dragon who provided the scales, plus [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to a damage type determined by that tradition: [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) for [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4). For instance, a dragonscale bo staff made with scales taken from an [omen dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2953) would provide a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against occult effects and resistance 5 to mental damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include scales from one type of dragon.

Dragon teeth line the edges of this _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [leiomano](/Weapons.aspx?ID=292)_ . The leiomano deals an additional 1d6 damage of a type determined by the tradition of the dragon from which the teeth were taken: [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) for [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4). The weapon also gains the relevant trait (for instance, fire for a [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) made with teeth taken from an [adamantine dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2932)).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You swing the leiomano, sending several of the dragon teeth shooting through the air on jets of energy. The dragon teeth deal 3d6 piercing damage and 3d6 damage of the damage type corresponding to the dragon’s tradition in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Reflex save). The teeth hunt down their targets, correcting their flight in midair, which reduces any circumstance bonus from [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) by 2.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include teeth from a dragon.

A grabbed creature can use the [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) action to get out of the Grab, and the Grab ends for a grabbed creature if you move away from it. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vine Whip](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1889)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 38

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)_ is crafted from the vine of a dangerous plant creature. It deals bludgeoning or slashing damage, according to the vine attack of the creature it was harvested from. For example, collecting a vine from an [assassin vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555) would result in a _vine whip_ that deals bludgeoning damage, while one from a [mandragora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=727) or [viper vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=854) would deal piercing damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** You have soil or sand within your reach; **Effect** You [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=83) the weapon and plant it in the ground, where it takes root and fights on its own against the last enemy you attacked or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated.

 The weapon has a space of 5 feet, acts on your initiative, and gains two actions. It doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide flanking. The weapon can't use reactions, and it acts on your turn.

 While Activated, a _vine whip_ makes Strikes with an attack modifier of +22 plus its item bonus to attack rolls (normally +24 total). It uses the weapon's normal damage but has a +0 Strength modifier. The whip's abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can't be Activated or benefit from any of your abilities while rooted.

 Each round, when the _vine whip_ is finished using its actions, attempt a DC 6 flat check. On a failure, the Activation ends and the _vine whip_ becomes inanimate. You can't Activate the item again for 10 minutes. Removing the _vine whip_ from the ground takes an Interact action.

 While rooted, the _vine whip_ gains [fast healing 1](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the vine from a plant creature with a vine attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vine Whip

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kaldemash's Lament](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1890)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Resembling little more than a simple iron pipe with a handle, _Kaldemash's Lament_ is one of the most well-known star guns in all of Arcadia. Legends state the Crowned Regent Kaldemash helped forge one of the first star guns millennia ago. While the star gun served Kaldemash as a powerful weapon, its most notable achievement was the accidental killing of one of Kaldemash's greatest friends. This death is what caused the regent to recognize the true destructive power of the star guns and led to him developing the Star Code, a set of rules of engagement and proper use of firearms still in use in Arcadia today. Although Kaldemash never named the weapon himself, all legends that mention the weapon refer to it as _Kaldemash's Lament_.

 The legendary weapon is a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [ quickstrike](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2844)_ advanced firearm with a range increment of 90 feet. It deals 4d6 force damage (with the _major striking_ rune included) and has the [concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=327), [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), and [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178) traits. You don't take a penalty when dealing nonlethal damage with the weapon. Like most star guns, _Kaldemash's Lament_ uses magic to function and doesn't require ammunition or [black powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1190).

 If you use _Kaldemash's Lament_ as part of a duel in which all parties are in agreement on the terms, the gun's supernatural instincts help you make the quickest draw. You roll twice and take the higher result on your initiative roll for the duel; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect. In addition, you can draw _Kaldemash's Lament_ as a free action at the start of your turn during the duel. If you attempt to fire the star gun in bad faith at a dueling opponent once they have surrendered, been defeated, or the duel is over, _Kaldemash's Lament_ flies out of your hand and you can't pick it up, hold it, or wield it for 10 minutes.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You target a creature with an attack;  **Effect** You adjust the damage that _Kaldemash's Lament_ deals before firing. _Kaldemash's Lament's_ damage type changes to either electricity, fire, or force until you change the type again.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You leave _Kaldemash's Lament_ to fire on its own. You release the star gun and it begins to move independently, flying through the air and firing. You still gain the benefits of the gun's [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) rune while it's moving independently. It has a space of 5 feet but doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move through that space. It always remains within 30 feet of you and intentionally resists being taken or otherwise moved; all attempts to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) it fail. _Kaldemash's Lament_ moves this way for 3 rounds, after which it returns to your hand. If you don't have a free hand to hold it, the gun instead holsters itself on your person.

 While _Kaldemash's Lament_ is moving independently, you can command it to make a Strike against a creature as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. It makes a ranged Strike using your attack modifier while wielding it or a +31 bonus, whichever is higher. This attack increases your multiple attack penalty as normal, and the gun uses your multiple attack penalty when determining its attack bonus. Since the star gun is constantly moving and attempting to line up the appropriate shot, creatures it targets are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the gun's attacks.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You unleash a barrage of attacks in an instant. You deal Strike damage to all creatures in a 30-foot cone (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). This barrage counts as three attacks for your multiple attack penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kaldemash's Lament

When fired, the spark gun deals mental damage and adds the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait to the attack. Each _mindlance_ also includes a [reinforced stock](/Weapons.aspx?ID=187) that benefits from any [fundamental runes](/Equipment.aspx?Category=23&Subcategory=25) on the firearm. <traits>

<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)_ features multiple triangular panels that can be folded out of the stock, like the petals of a tigridia flower, that collect sunlight and feed it into the spark gun's core. All damage dealt by a _solar shellflower_ is fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day (but see below);   **Effect** One of the panels from the _solar shellflower_ detaches and unfurls into a tigridia-shaped construct of flame. For the next 1 minute, whenever you Strike an enemy with the _solar shellflower_, the construct fires a smaller jet of fire that automatically hits the target, dealing 3d4 additional [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) or twice that on a critical hit. The _solar shellflower_ usually requires a full day to replenish enough solar energy to recharge this Activation, but if used in an environment with especially strong sunlight, such as in a desert, cliff above the clouds, or near the summer solstice, it can recharge in 1 hour instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Solar Shellflower

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [pepperbox](/Weapons.aspx?ID=205)_ cycles through several magical cores, swapping in new ones while the previous ones cool. The gun deals fire or electricity damage, alternating with each attack as it rotates cores. Arcadian gunslingers liken the rotating cores to a group of dancers, each taking their turn in the spotlight.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _spark dancer_ casts either [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) or [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), depending on whether the current core would deal fire or electricity damage, as a 7th-level arcane spell (DC 30).

Monster hunters favor bane ammunition that contains a capsule of reagents tailored to a particular type of creature— [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=79), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), or both [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125). <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bane Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1895)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Monster hunters favor bane ammunition that contains a capsule of reagents tailored to a particular type of creature— [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=79), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), or both [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125). Each type requires a different formula. When activated bane ammunition hits a target that has a trait matching the selected type, it takes [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) in addition to the damage the attack normally deals.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blister Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1896)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Blister ammunition is loaded with alchemically processed irritants, such as pollen, pepper, and formic acid. A creature hit by activated blister ammunition must attempt a Fortitude save or begin to itch uncontrollably in addition to damage the attack normally deals. On a critical hit, increase the Fortitude DC by 2, and the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. For the duration, each time the target attempts a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) action, it must attempt a DC 8 flat check, losing the action on a failure. An affected creature can use a single Interact action to scratch and sneeze, allowing it to automatically pass the flat check. The effect ends early once an affected creature spends 3 Interact actions scratching and sneezing. These Interact actions don't need to be consecutive.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

**Price** 21 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The maximum duration is 3 rounds, and the Fortitude DC is 19.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The maximum duration is 6 rounds, and the Fortitude DC is 28.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The maximum duration is 10 rounds, and the Fortitude DC is 35.
name: Blister Ammunition

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, the reservoir of alchemical reagents in elemental ammunition atomizes on impact, dealing [persistent acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the target and splash damage in addition to the damage the attack normally deals. Each damage type requires a different formula, and the ammunition gains a trait matching the damage type.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Freeze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1898)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 42

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Freeze ammunition carries chilling reagents that activate on contact with the target. A creature hit by activated freeze ammunition takes cold damage instead of the weapon's normal damage type, plus 2 cold splash damage. Hitting a 5-foot-square surface successfully with freeze ammunition deals 2 cold splash damage and covers the space in a layer of ice. Each creature standing on the icy surface must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check or else fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). Creatures using an action to move onto the icy surface must attempt either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28). Creatures that Step or [Crawl](/Actions.aspx?ID=76) don't need to attempt a check or save. The ice melts after 1 minute, although unusually hot or cold temperatures can change this duration at the GM's discretion. Dealing at least 1 point of fire damage to the ice removes it instantly

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Freeze Ammunition

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Life Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1899)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Life shot is a special cartridge that carries a small dose of [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91). A creature hit by activated life shot takes no damage from the successful attack, instead receiving healing and gaining an item bonus to saving throws against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) for 1 minute. On a critical hit, roll the healing received twice and take the better result (this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect). A target willing to be hit by this attack is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against it.

<title

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Life Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1899)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Life shot is a special cartridge that carries a small dose of [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91). A creature hit by activated life shot takes no damage from the successful attack, instead receiving healing and gaining an item bonus to saving throws against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) for 1 minute. On a critical hit, roll the healing received twice and take the better result (this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect). A target willing to be hit by this attack is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against it.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1900)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ooze ammunition is a capsule containing a sticky substance. If you hit a creature with activated ooze ammunition, it deals acid damage instead of its normal damage type, and the creature then takes the listed penalty to Speed and [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) until it ends the effects. On a critical hit, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round in addition to the other effects. The target can end the effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) the sticky foam. Other creatures can provide the actions, although doing so deals half the ammunition's persistent acid damage to the assisting creature. A creature that ends the effect still takes the persistent damage that turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs=""
>
Ooze Ammunition (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 1d4 persistent acid damage and imposes a –5-foot penalty to Speed, and the Escape DC is 16.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs=""
>
Ooze Ammunition (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

**Price** 42 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 2d4 persistent acid damage and imposes a –10-foot penalty to Speed, and the Escape DC is 20.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs=""
>
Ooze Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 3d4 persistent acid damage and imposes a –10-foot penalty to Speed, and the Escape DC is 29.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1900)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ooze ammunition is a capsule containing a sticky substance. If you hit a creature with activated ooze ammunition, it deals acid damage instead of its normal damage type, and the creature then takes the listed penalty to Speed and [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) until it ends the effects. On a critical hit, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round in addition to the other effects. The target can end the effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) the sticky foam. Other creatures can provide the actions, although doing so deals half the ammunition's persistent acid damage to the assisting creature. A creature that ends the effect still takes the persistent damage that turn.

    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rusting Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1901)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rusting ammunition is made using a [ore louse's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2665) saliva and, when activated, deals damage to objects or creatures primarily made of metal. The target takes [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) for a duration determined by the type of ammunition used. A creature that drops to 0 Hit Points while taking this persistent damage crumbles into fine powder; its gear remains. The ammunition's type determines the maximum amount of an object that's destroyed.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the saliva of an ore louse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Limited"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 3d8 persistent damage for up to 5 rounds. For an object, the ammunition destroys a 5-foot cube.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Rusting Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 43

**Price** 3,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 4d8 persistent damage for up to 6 rounds. For an object, the ammunition destroys a 10-foot cube.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blightburn Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1902)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Blightburn bombs have radioactive materials sealed inside flasks treated with lead. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals poison damage, [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and poison [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage, according to the bomb's type. A creature that takes the persistent poison damage deals the splash damage again from its current position as the radiation continues to harm nearby creatures. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boulder Seed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1903)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bomb is made of volatile fluids that rapidly expand and harden when exposed to air. A boulder seed grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals bludgeoning damage and bludgeoning splash damage, according to the bomb's type. When activated, the bomb fills a 5-foot cube with hardened foam, which has stats according to the bomb's type and which [pushes](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) a primary target of a certain size or smaller occupying that space 5 feet away from you. On a critical hit, the target also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The splash zone fills with rubble, creating difficult terrain. The “boulder” the bomb creates fails all saving throws and loses 1 Hardness per round, disintegrating into fine powder when the boulder's Hardness is reduced to 0. At that time, the difficult terrain the bomb created also disappears.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boulder Seed
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d4 bludgeoning damage plus 3 bludgeoning splash damage. It creates a boulder as hard as wood (Hardness 5, HP 20) that pushes Medium or smaller targets.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boulder Seed (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

**Price** 3,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d4 bludgeoning damage plus 4 bludgeoning splash damage. It creates a boulder as hard as stone (Hardness 10, HP 40) that pushes Large or smaller targets.
name: Boulder Seed

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Lodestone bombs hold reactive ionized minerals preserved in a dormant state until broken. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals force damage and force splash damage, according to the bomb's type. In addition, a target made of metal, wearing metal armor, or using metal weapons takes [persistent force damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while taking the persistent damage. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

</row>

---

Lodestone bombs hold reactive ionized minerals preserved in a dormant state until broken. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals force damage and force splash damage, according to the bomb's type. In addition, a target made of metal, wearing metal armor, or using metal weapons takes [persistent force damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while taking the persistent damage. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shatterstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1905)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A shatterstone is a small ceramic orb. Inside are reactive agents that set up an intense field of sonic vibration when the stone breaks. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals sonic damage and sonic splash damage, according to the bomb's type. Much of the sound is ultrasonic, and creatures with sonic weakness that take damage from the bomb must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw at the listed DC or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) until the end of their next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shatterstone
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

**Price** 380 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d6 sonic damage and 3 sonic splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 30.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shatterstone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 44

**Price** 3,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d6 sonic damage and 4 sonic splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 40.
name: Shatterstone

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- Skunk bombs are made from the concentrated odors of [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102), [hezrous](/Monsters.aspx?ID=601), and other creatures with natural or supernatural stench. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals poison damage and poison splash damage. Any creature hit by the bomb or in its splash area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw with a DC based on the bomb's type. Creatures in the splash area treat the results of their saving throw as one step better. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The target is sickened 1 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) while sickened.
**Critical Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, sickened 2, and slowed 1 while sickened. Creatures sickened by the bomb emit an odor that lasts 10 minutes after the sickened condition ends (or 1 hour if they were also blinded). The odor can be removed or neutralized by using [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) or similar magic or by spending 10 minutes scrubbing with ample soap and water. While the odor lasts, creatures within 30 feet can smell the target, enabling even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) or [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) doubles the range at which it can detect the target using this scent. Skunk Bomb (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45 **Price** 4 gp **Bulk** L --- The bomb deals 1d4 poison damage and 1 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 15. Skunk Bomb (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45 **Price** 12 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 poison damage and 2 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 17. Skunk Bomb (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45 **Price** 240 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 poison damage and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 28. Skunk Bomb (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45 **Price** 2,400 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 poison damage and 4 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 37.
name: Skunk Bomb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trueshape Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1907)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 45

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Concentrated wolfsbane and other anti-shapechanger reagents fill trueshape bombs. These bombs grant an item bonus to attack rolls and deal poison damage, [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and poison splash damage, according to their type. If the primary target is under the effects of a [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113) or [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effect, it must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw at the bomb's listed DC, or else the effects end and the creature returns to its normal form. Targets taking persistent poison damage from this bomb must succeed at another Fortitude saving throw at the same DC to change shape using a morph or polymorph effect. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d6 poison damage, 3d4 persistent poison damage, and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 30.

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d6 poison damage, 4d4 persistent poison damage, and 4 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 40.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arbor Wine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1908)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Watering grape or berry bushes with certain alchemical liquids as they grow results in a ruby-red drink that’s rich and heady. The vines develop a connection to their natural environment and impart it onto the wine’s imbiber. For 1 minute after drinking a glass of arbor wine, you have [tremorsense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2413) at a range of 30 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arbor Wine
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arbor Wine (Aged)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Arbor wine that has been aged for several years—or artificially aged with alchemy—develops both a greater complexity of flavor and a stronger connection to nature. When you drink aged arbor wine, vegetation in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you begins to writhe, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the start of your next turn.
name: Arbor Wine

Watering grape or berry bushes with certain alchemical liquids as they grow results in a ruby-red drink that’s rich and heady. The vines develop a connection to their natural environment and impart it onto the wine’s imbiber. For 1 minute after drinking a glass of arbor wine, you have [tremorsense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2413) at a range of 30 feet.

[Auric Noodles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1909)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

</row>

</column>

---

Auric noodles are boiled, then pan-fried and tossed with sliced vegetables and a sticky, savory sauce incorporating alchemical reagents. After you eat the noodles, they boost your ability to sense magic for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), and you can move at full speed while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=513) exploration activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Auric Noodles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cinnamon Seers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1910)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Zippy, alchemically treated cinnamon suffuses cinnamon seers, a rock candy with a lively taste that provides a mental boost. A cinnamon seer remains in your mouth for 1 hour, its stimulating flavor granting you a +1 item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Trigger** You gain no information from a Recall Knowledge check; **Effect** Reroll the triggering check. If this was a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check, the GM rerolls rather than you; the candy doesn't give you any insight into what the GM rolled, so in that case, you're rerolling based only on guesswork. The seer becomes inert. You become temporarily immune to cinnamon seers until the next time you make your daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cinnamon Seers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cooperative Waffles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1911)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Flash-cooked on a [waffle iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1403) and drizzled with alchemical syrups and compound butter, cooperative waffles bolster the supportive spirit of those who share the batch. You can split the waffles with one other creature. After you both eat half of the waffles to Activate them, when one of you uses [Follow the Expert](/Actions.aspx?ID=514) to follow the other, the circumstance bonus granted is 1 higher. The waffles' bonus lasts 24 hours or until you next make your daily preparations, whichever comes first. You can only be linked to one creature in this way at a time; if either of you eats cooperative waffles again, the effect of your previous waffles ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

The benefit of the waffles also applies when one of you successfully [Aids](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) a skill check the other attempts.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crackling Bubble Gum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1912)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tangy gum cracks and pops in your mouth as you chew it. While you're chewing crackling bubble gum, for up to 10 minutes, you have an item bonus to saving throws against auditory and sonic effects.

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Effect** You blow a bubble with the gum until it pops, after which it becomes inert. The pop deals sonic damage to all creatures in a 15-foot cone with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also bound with sticky gum, taking a –10-foot item penalty to its Speed for 1 minute. The creature can remove the gum with a total of 3 Interact actions. These actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tangy gum cracks and pops in your mouth as you chew it. While you're chewing crackling bubble gum, for up to 10 minutes, you have an item bonus to saving throws against auditory and sonic effects.

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Effect** You blow a bubble with the gum until it pops, after which it becomes inert. The pop deals sonic damage to all creatures in a 15-foot cone with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also bound with sticky gum, taking a –10-foot item penalty to its Speed for 1 minute. The creature can remove the gum with a total of 3 Interact actions. These actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Delve Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1913)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

If fried, cast-off scales of a [benthic worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2872) render into crunchy snacks. [Alchemists](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) add reagents to the frying oil to enhance the scales’ properties and flavor. For 1 minute after eating a delve scale, you gain a burrow Speed of 15 feet and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Delve Scale

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diplomat's Charcuterie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1914)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A common sight at political gatherings, a diplomat's charcuterie has fine meats, cheeses, nuts, fruits, and other finger foods mixed with reagents to engender friendly feelings between those consuming them. Contents of the plate vary by chef and the intended palates, from the hearty sausages and hard cheese of charcuterie from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to the hot-pepper cheese curds and smoked almonds of Thuvian platters. After Activating the charcuterie by eating it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) and Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85). These bonuses last 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diplomat's Charcuterie

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 27 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Containing neither eggs nor cream, an egg cream fizz contains milk or nut milk, sparkling water, and flavored syrup, frothed and chilled. Upon drinking, you feel lighter and more buoyant, gaining a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed for 10 minutes. During this time, you also gain another effect determined by the drink's syrup, which is chosen when the drink is created.
  • **Chocolate** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28), [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30), and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31).
  • **Strawberry** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).
  • **Vanilla** You can Step into difficult terrain.
name: Egg Cream Fizz

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Firefoot Popcorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1916)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you consume firefoot popcorn, for 1 minute, you can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) double the normal distance. You can also attempt to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) as a single action. If you do, you don't perform the initial Stride (nor do you fall if you don't Stride 10 feet).

 Each time you Leap 10 feet or more, your feet make a popping sound that can be heard from at least 100 feet, and the space you left fills with waves of intense heat. For 1 minute, that square is [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454) that deals 1d6 fire damage to any creature that moves into it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Firefoot Popcorn

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- A fury cocktail is a fortifying ginger beer spiked with rum and a mixer. It's rumored to have originated from a barbarian-themed festival in a popular mead hall specializing in alchemical beverages. **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to melee attack rolls and an additional effect depending on the additive chosen when the brew is created.
  • **Animalistic** Lemon juice and powdered claws or talons are added to the cocktail. You gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group of your choice between a jaws attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage or a claw attack that has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) trait and deals 1d4 slashing damage.
  • **Double** This cocktail is just stronger, with more ginger and more rum. You gain resistance 1 to physical damage. The resistance increases to 2 for a moderate fury cocktail or 5 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Mournful** A few flower petals add a powerful aroma to the drink. You gain resistance 1 to void damage, or resistance 1 to vitality damage if you have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) ability. The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Skeptical** A splash of bitters gives the drink a more complex flavor. You gain a +1 item bonus to saves against magic. This bonus is +2 for a moderate fury cocktail or +3 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Titanic** Yuzu juice and powdered giant hair are added to this cocktail. If you're Medium or smaller, you gain the following effects: you become Large, are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), and increase your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out Tiny).
  • **Wyrmhide** Pomegranate juice and elemental reagents are added to the cocktail. You gain resistance 1 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and poison damage. The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves. Fury Cocktail (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 15 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +2 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 10 minutes. Fury Cocktail (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 360 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +3 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour. Fury Cocktail (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 4,000 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +4 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour.
name: Fury Cocktail

summary_markdown: A fury cocktail is a fortifying ginger beer spiked with rum and a mixer. It's rumored to have originated from a barbarian-themed festival in a …

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- A fury cocktail is a fortifying ginger beer spiked with rum and a mixer. It's rumored to have originated from a barbarian-themed festival in a popular mead hall specializing in alchemical beverages. **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to melee attack rolls and an additional effect depending on the additive chosen when the brew is created.
  • **Animalistic** Lemon juice and powdered claws or talons are added to the cocktail. You gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group of your choice between a jaws attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage or a claw attack that has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) trait and deals 1d4 slashing damage.
  • **Double** This cocktail is just stronger, with more ginger and more rum. You gain resistance 1 to physical damage. The resistance increases to 2 for a moderate fury cocktail or 5 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Mournful** A few flower petals add a powerful aroma to the drink. You gain resistance 1 to void damage, or resistance 1 to vitality damage if you have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) ability. The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Skeptical** A splash of bitters gives the drink a more complex flavor. You gain a +1 item bonus to saves against magic. This bonus is +2 for a moderate fury cocktail or +3 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Titanic** Yuzu juice and powdered giant hair are added to this cocktail. If you're Medium or smaller, you gain the following effects: you become Large, are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), and increase your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out Tiny).
  • **Wyrmhide** Pomegranate juice and elemental reagents are added to the cocktail. You gain resistance 1 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and poison damage. The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves. Fury Cocktail (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 15 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +2 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 10 minutes. Fury Cocktail (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 360 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +3 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour. Fury Cocktail (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 4,000 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +4 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour.
name: Fury Cocktail (Lesser)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Galvanic Chew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1918)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A galvanic chew is a processed ball of dried [electric eel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=174), roasted until chewy and coated in spicy, reagent-infused powder that tingles with electricity as you chew. For up to 1 hour, you have resistance 5 to electricity.

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) **Trigger** You're hit by a melee attack or touched by a creature; **Effect** You channel electricity into the triggering creature, which must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or stunned 2 on a critical failure). The chew becomes inert. You're then temporarily immune to galvanic chews for 24 hours or until the next time you make your daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Galvanic Chew

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ginger Chew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1919)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 49

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This chewy ginger candy aids digestion and soothes unsettled stomachs. A ginger chew lasts for 1 hour and grants you a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves against being [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Effect** Reduce your sickened value by 1, if any. The chew becomes inert.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ginger Chew

For 1 hour after you drink an insight coffee, you use d8s instead of d6s for your extra damage from the strategic strike class feature, if you have it. You also gain an item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge with a skill determined by the blend chosen when the item is crafted.

  • **Hazelnut** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)
  • **Mocha** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)
  • **Vanilla** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)
  • **Pumpkin Spice** [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)
  • **Toffee** [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)
