<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Journeybread](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1921)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 49

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Journeybread contains a mix of fruits, nuts, and grains with an alchemical boost. Eating one journeybread provides all the food and water you need for a day. If you subsist on nothing else for a week, you become temporarily immune to journeybread until you eat real food and drink water normally for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mender's Soup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1922)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 49

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hot, broth-based soup typically contains tubers, leeks, zesty spices, reagents, and if desired, the meat of livestock. Civic authorities commission batches of mender's soup for workers if a tricky job is on the agenda. After you eat the soup, its effects last 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) and restore an additional 5 Hit Points to items you successfully Repair during this time. 

If you eat mender's soup over the entire period required to attempt a Crafting check to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) (typically a minimum of eating the soup each day for 4 days), the +1 bonus from the soup can be applied to that check, too.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mender's Soup

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Missive Mint](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1923)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 49

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This white mint with a chalky coating appears to be a normal candy unless someone examining it succeeds at a DC 15 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check to [Identify Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=44). If the crafter has the powerful alchemy class feature, this DC is their class DC instead, if it's higher. The mint's crafter can imbue a missive mint with a message containing up to 25 words while creating it. Someone who consumes the missive mint hears the message in a fizzing voice as the mint's coating bubbles away, which takes the same amount of time as it would to speak the message. The mint's eater has no way of knowing who the original sender was, what they sound like, or who the message was intended for.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Missive Mint

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Not only are the eggs of giant owls delicious when boiled, but when infused with a mix of alchemical reagents, they also make you emit a long and terrifying screech. All creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 23 Will save. Regardless of the result, creatures in the area are temporarily immune to this screech for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 round.
name: Owl Screech Egg

[Phantom Roll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1925)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

</column>

---

A phantom roll contains vegetables, greens, and fine, clear noodles, all wrapped in transparent, edible starch paper and alchemically treated and laced with a tangy sauce. Upon eating the roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you attempt during the [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=511) exploration activity. You can also Avoid Notice at full Speed or combine it with [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) or [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=518) while moving at half Speed. These effects expire 24 hours after you eat the roll or when you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Roll

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />

</row>

</column>

---

Made from a special mixture of honey and alchemical reagents, poison fizz is a zesty, sweet rock candy that pops and crackles in your mouth. For 1 hour, you have an item bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and being [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Effect** You bite the poison fizz to release its poisonous liquid center and spray green mist in a 15-foot cone. This deals poison damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the end of your next turn and is then temporarily immune to being blinded by poison fizz for 1 hour.

</row>

</column>

---

Made from a special mixture of honey and alchemical reagents, poison fizz is a zesty, sweet rock candy that pops and crackles in your mouth. For 1 hour, you have an item bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and being [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Effect** You bite the poison fizz to release its poisonous liquid center and spray green mist in a 15-foot cone. This deals poison damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the end of your next turn and is then temporarily immune to being blinded by poison fizz for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pucker Pickle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1927)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp and pungent, but tasty, pucker pickles were created by [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) [alchemists](/Classes.aspx?ID=1) working to avoid being eaten by larger creatures. For 1 hour after eating a pucker pickle, you smell slightly of pickle, but you have a horrendous taste. Once a creature hits you with a Strike using an attack that allows it to taste you, such as a jaws Strike, it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to further attacks against you that allow it to taste you, including attacks like [Grappling](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Tripping](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you using its jaws or [Swallowing you Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35). Creatures, especially [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), often choose other targets after tasting you. Any creature that [Engulfs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) you or Swallows you Whole is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). If it spends an action retching to reduce the sickened condition, you can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) as a reaction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pucker Pickle

>
[Saboteur's Friend](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1928)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The euphemistically named saboteur's friend looks, smells, and tastes like an appetizing chocolate square. You lace the chocolate with reagents that induce a strong laxative effect. Saboteur's friend is useful for incapacitating rather than dealing lasting harm. Unlike some poisons, saboteur's friend can have its sickened condition reduced (but changing to a higher or lower stage after a save applies any sickened condition listed for that stage, as normal).

 **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (10 minutes); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and sickened 2 (10 minutes); **Stage 3** enfeebled 1, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), and sickened 3 (10 minutes)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Saboteur's Friend

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scholar's Drop](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1929)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

University students in Katapesh first used the scholar's drop to gain an edge over their academic rivals, but the candy has since spread across Golarion. The flavor of this hard, sugar-coated candy is highly refreshing and based on lemon and green tea. For 1 hour, you gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects that could render you [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Requirements** You're fatigued; **Effect** Ignore the effects of the fatigued condition for 10 minutes. The drop's other effects end for you, and when the 10 minutes are up, you're temporarily immune to scholar's drops for 1 hour. If you use this effect three times in a single day, you become temporarily immune to scholar's drops entirely until you get a full night's rest.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scholar's Drop

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silver Crescent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1930)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A piquant tamarind and chili-lime flavor infuses a silver crescent, which was first created to aid those battling the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). For 1 hour, you shed cool, white light like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), and you gain an item bonus to saving throws against [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246) effects according to the crescent's type. While shedding this light, you can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if you're visible, and if you're [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you're concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Effect** A ray of light descends on a 5-foot square of your choice within 120 feet. Any creature in that space takes positive damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn on a failed save. This is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. The silver crescent becomes inert.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Silver Crescent (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, the DC is 20, and the ray deals 4d6 damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Silver Crescent (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 235 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, the DC is 28, and the ray deals 7d6 damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Silver Crescent (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, the DC is 35, and the ray deals 9d6 damage.
name: Silver Crescent

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silver Crescent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1930)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A piquant tamarind and chili-lime flavor infuses a silver crescent, which was first created to aid those battling the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). For 1 hour, you shed cool, white light like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), and you gain an item bonus to saving throws against [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246) effects according to the crescent's type. While shedding this light, you can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if you're visible, and if you're [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you're concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Effect** A ray of light descends on a 5-foot square of your choice within 120 feet. Any creature in that space takes positive damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn on a failed save. This is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. The silver crescent becomes inert.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51 **Price** 45 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Hot tea with a comforting, flowery aroma, a soothing toddy grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects and against effects with a trait determined by the liquor mixed into the tea when it's created. These benefits last for 1 hour.
  • **Amaretto** [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)
  • **Limoncello** [Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)
  • **Whiskey** [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sprite Apple](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1932)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sparkling candy coating covers a sprite apple. For 10 minutes after consuming a sprite apple, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). While shedding this light, you can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if you're visible, and if you're [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you're concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The light matches the vibrant color of the apple's candy coating. Creatures in the bright light are subject to another effect, depending on the type of apple.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sprite Apple (Chartreuse)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

**Price** 26 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel more in tune with nature, gaining a +1 item bonus to Nature checks and to Diplomacy checks attempted against animals or plants (if they have the [Animal Empathy](/Feats.aspx?ID=4709) feat, [Plant Empathy](/Feats.aspx?ID=4711) feat, or some other ability to make such checks).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sprite Apple (Golden)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel kind and inviting, taking a –1 item penalty to their Will DC against [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sprite Apple (Pink)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel a burst of creativity, gaining a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sprite Apple (Teal)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

**Price** 41 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel a sense of calm, gaining a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects.
name: Sprite Apple

A sparkling candy coating covers a sprite apple. For 10 minutes after consuming a sprite apple, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). While shedding this light, you can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if you're visible, and if you're [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you're concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The light matches the vibrant color of the apple's candy coating. Creatures in the bright light are subject to another effect, depending on the type of apple.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

<column gap="tiny">

---

Alchemical reagents add punch to tracker's stew: a rich, fiery mixture of tomatoes, ground nuts, onions, and tubers, often with poultry added. It's usually served with or over rice or noodles. Once you've eaten the stew, it improves your ability to sense and follow tracks for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66). You can do either while moving at full speed or both while moving at half speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

---

Shaped like a pointed, gnarled finger on a stick, witch's finger is a frozen treat imbued with berries that lend it a blood-red hue. A popular tale claims Irriseni winter witches created this dessert, but the story is apocryphal; an enterprising ice cream shop owner in New Stetven invented the treat and, as a marketing ploy, the myth surrounding it. Taking a bite makes you feel warm. For 1 hour, you have cold resistance 3, and for 8 hours, the treat negates the damage you would take from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) and reduces the damage you take from extreme cold to that of severe cold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Witch's Finger

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Repellent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1936)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal repellent is a noxious alchemical substance that makes you repulsive to a certain kind of [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). You can, for example, make [ape](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=6) repellent, [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=13) repellent, or [snake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=93) repellent, but not a repellent that affects all animals. The repellent doesn't work on creatures that are similar to the kind of animal, but not actually animals—for example, bear repellent wouldn't work on [werebears](/Monsters.aspx?ID=412). Animal repellent is ineffective against animals with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher, such as [awakened animals](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3). When you initially learn the formula for animal repellent, you learn the formulas for all common animals. If no animals of a kind are common, such as [sea serpents](/Monsters.aspx?ID=361), you must learn the formula for that kind separately, and it has the same rarity as the least-rare creature of that kind.

 You Activate animal repellent by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, or by spreading it on a surface in an area up to 10 feet square. The repellent lasts for 24 hours or until scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work (consisting of multiple Interact actions). The designated kind of animal can smell the repellent from three times as far away as normal.

 The designated kind of creature typically avoids the coated creature or area, which smells foul and feels unnerving to it. If in combat or otherwise distressed, the creature can approach within 20 feet of the affected creature or area only if it succeeds at a Will save with a DC determined by the type of animal repellent. Once it succeeds at this save, the animal is temporarily immune for 1 minute. If the animal attacks in a way that causes it to taste or ingest the repellent, it must succeed at a Fortitude save at the listed DC or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or sickened 2 on a critical failure).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Repellent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1936)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal repellent is a noxious alchemical substance that makes you repulsive to a certain kind of [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). You can, for example, make [ape](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=6) repellent, [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=13) repellent, or [snake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=93) repellent, but not a repellent that affects all animals. The repellent doesn't work on creatures that are similar to the kind of animal, but not actually animals—for example, bear repellent wouldn't work on [werebears](/Monsters.aspx?ID=412). Animal repellent is ineffective against animals with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher, such as [awakened animals](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3). When you initially learn the formula for animal repellent, you learn the formulas for all common animals. If no animals of a kind are common, such as [sea serpents](/Monsters.aspx?ID=361), you must learn the formula for that kind separately, and it has the same rarity as the least-rare creature of that kind.

 You Activate animal repellent by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, or by spreading it on a surface in an area up to 10 feet square. The repellent lasts for 24 hours or until scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work (consisting of multiple Interact actions). The designated kind of animal can smell the repellent from three times as far away as normal.

 The designated kind of creature typically avoids the coated creature or area, which smells foul and feels unnerving to it. If in combat or otherwise distressed, the creature can approach within 20 feet of the affected creature or area only if it succeeds at a Will save with a DC determined by the type of animal repellent. Once it succeeds at this save, the animal is temporarily immune for 1 minute. If the animal attacks in a way that causes it to taste or ingest the repellent, it must succeed at a Fortitude save at the listed DC or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or sickened 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Dye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1937)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Camouflage dye uses a variety of alchemically treated paints and crushed crystals to make the user particularly hard to distinguish from their surroundings. When you Activate the dye by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, the target and its clothing change colors, blending into their surroundings until the target makes a sudden movement. The target can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for 10 minutes. If the target uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) or moves at more than half its Speed, after that action is completed, the effects of camouflage powder end and the creature ceases to be [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Camouflage Dye (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title

>
Camouflage Dye (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The dye lasts 1 minute, and the effects don't end if the target uses a hostile action or moves at more than half its Speed.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Colorful Coating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1938)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It's possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM's discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Blue)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that's loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface's collapse.

 This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the coated surface.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to Balance of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object's Hardness as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating's surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as in an area where gravity is reversed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is difficult terrain. Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20) or spending 2 Interact actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square's surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces difficult terrain caused by slipperiness, such as from ice or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456) to greater difficult terrain. The coating can't affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface's terrain.

 The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Climb the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It's possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM's discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Blue)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that's loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface's collapse.

 This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the coated surface.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to Balance of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object's Hardness as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating's surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as in an area where gravity is reversed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is difficult terrain. Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20) or spending 2 Interact actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square's surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces difficult terrain caused by slipperiness, such as from ice or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456) to greater difficult terrain. The coating can't affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface's terrain.

 The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Climb the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating (Indigo)

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to Balance of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object's Hardness as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating's surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as in an area where gravity is reversed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is difficult terrain. Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20) or spending 2 Interact actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 52

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square's surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces difficult terrain caused by slipperiness, such as from ice or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456) to greater difficult terrain. The coating can't affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface's terrain.

 The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Climb the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating (Violet)

</row>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It's possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM's discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that's loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface's collapse.

 This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the coated surface.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emetic Paste](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1939)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 53

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) creatures have difficulty swallowing, so you can Activate emetic paste by applying it to your skin or that of a sickened creature within reach, typically on the throat. The paste makes it easy for the sickened creature to purge, granting it an immediate Fortitude save to reduce its sickened condition. The paste grants the target an item bonus to that save and to all saving throws to reduce the sickened condition for 1 hour.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Gearbinder oil comes in a sealed pump that can squirt the oil a short distance. The oil is designed to flow through complex mechanisms and, agitated through mechanical action, foam up and form a paste that binds the works. The oil is effective against articulated constructs and machinery, including many [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), [clockworks](/Traits.aspx?ID=331), and [mechanical](/Traits.aspx?ID=105) hazards. You apply the oil to the target you want to bind, which must be within 10 feet of you. After the oil is applied, at the end of any round during which the target took an action with the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait, it must attempt a Fortitude save against a DC determined by the oil's type. A mechanism that's [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) or more by gearbinder oil also can't use reactions. Gearbinder oil functions for up to 6 rounds before becoming an inert, oily residue. **Critical Success** The oil becomes inert, and the effect ends.
**Success** The target reduces its slowed condition by 1. If the slowed condition's value is 0, the effect ends.
**Failure** The target increases its slowed condition by 1, to a maximum of slowed 3.
**Critical Failure** The target increases its slowed condition by 2, to a maximum of slowed 3. Gearbinder Oil (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 45 gp **Bulk** L --- The DC for saves is 20. Gearbinder Oil (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 390 gp **Bulk** L --- The DC for saves is 29. Gearbinder Oil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 4,500 gp **Bulk** L --- The DC for saves is 38.
name: Gearbinder Oil

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Healing Vapor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1941)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Healing vapor is a substance that accelerates natural recovery processes by dispersing a mist infused with a variety of reagents typically used for healing and recovery. When deployed from a sealed container, the vapors fill a 5-foot burst, last for 10 minutes, and can affect up to four living creatures at one time. Any creatures beyond the first four gain no benefit, though if a creature leaves before the duration is over, a new creature that enters can benefit from the mist. A creature benefiting from the vapors regains a number of Hit Points based on the vapor's type. While affected, a creature also gains an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons. If the areas of more than one healing vapor overlap, only the strongest applies to creatures inside overlapping areas. Strong wind disperses the mist, rendering it ineffective while the wind blows.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healing Vapor (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Each creature regains 1 Hit Point every 2 minutes and gets a +1 bonus.

level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healing Vapor (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Each creature regains 1 Hit Point every minute and gets a +1 bonus.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healing Vapor (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Each creature regains 2 Hit Points every minute and gets a +2 bonus.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healing Vapor (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Each creature regains 3 Hit Points every minute and gets a +3 bonus.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Healing Vapor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1941)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Healing vapor is a substance that accelerates natural recovery processes by dispersing a mist infused with a variety of reagents typically used for healing and recovery. When deployed from a sealed container, the vapors fill a 5-foot burst, last for 10 minutes, and can affect up to four living creatures at one time. Any creatures beyond the first four gain no benefit, though if a creature leaves before the duration is over, a new creature that enters can benefit from the mist. A creature benefiting from the vapors regains a number of Hit Points based on the vapor's type. While affected, a creature also gains an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons. If the areas of more than one healing vapor overlap, only the strongest applies to creatures inside overlapping areas. Strong wind disperses the mist, rendering it ineffective while the wind blows.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Sealed in a packet so it can be released in a pressurized puff, implosion dust causes amorphous creatures to compress and shrink by hardening and even evaporating the liquid components of their physical forms. It's effective at weakening [water elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=45), [air elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=41), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), and other creatures the GM determines are similarly amorphous, which can even include particularly gelatinous [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=1). You release the dust toward one creature within 5 feet of you, which must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. Implosion dust functions for up to 6 rounds. It then becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size. **Critical Success** The dust becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size.
**Success** The target increases in size one step, up to its normal size. If it's at its normal size after the increase, the effect ends.
**Failure** The target decreases in size one step, to a minimum of Tiny. The [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) of each of its Strikes decreases by 5 feet, to a minimum of 5 feet if the creature is Small or larger, or a minimum of 0 feet if the creature is Tiny. Shrinking also imposes a status penalty to the creature's physical melee damage. This penalty is –2 for every step lower than its normal size the creature is.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target decreases in size by two steps. Implosion Dust (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 40 gp **Bulk** L --- The save DC is 20. Implosion Dust (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 370 gp **Bulk** L --- The save DC is 29. Implosion Dust (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54 **Price** 4,100 gp **Bulk** L --- The save DC is 38.
name: Implosion Dust

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Kept in an airtight spray bottle, revealing mist is an alchemical concoction that creates a sticky and clinging mist of chemicals in a 15-foot cone when sprayed. It doesn't affect visibility but causes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures in the area to be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). Revealing mist is ineffective in water or in areas with other factors affecting the spread of the mist, as determined by the GM. It remains in the area for 1 minute or until any significant wind disperses it, whichever comes first.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sense-Dulling Hood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1944)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes, an enemy’s dangerous special ability makes relatively standard sensory capabilities a liability. From an [ofalth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3117) emitting its stench to a [banshee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2845) wailing, plenty of creatures use their prey’s senses against them. A sensedulling hood is a wide, single-use mask designed to be pulled from an airtight package and slipped over the head. The mask grants you an item bonus to saving throws against [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effects for a time, according to the mask’s type. Since it dulls your senses without depriving you of them, the mask also imposes a –1 penalty to rolls and checks using [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) for the same duration.

</column>

---

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Soothing Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1945)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Soothing powders are remedies made to stop a particular type of [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). Each damage type requires a different formula, with the most popular being bleed soothing powder, poison soothing powder, fire soothing powder, and acid soothing powder. You Activate soothing powder by sprinkling it on yourself or another creature within reach. The target can immediately attempt a new flat check to remove persistent damage the powder works against. This powder lowers the DC to 10, as normal for a particularly appropriate type of help.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Soothing Powder (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Soothing Powder (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the effects described above, the powder persists for 1 minute, lowering the DCs of flat checks against that type of persistent damage due to particularly effective assistance.
name: Soothing Powder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sparkler](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1946)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 54

**Price** 4 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Sparklers are common in Tian Xia and Vudra and can be  Crafted  by those who can specifically Craft fireworks (such as a  fireworks technician ).

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sparkler gives off colorful sparks, burning for 1 minute. It provides [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 10-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 10 feet). While the sparkler burns, you can use it as an [improvised weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=224), dealing 1 fire damage on a hit. On a critical hit, you cause the target to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.

 You can touch a sparkler to a flammable object as part of the same Interact action you use to Activate it or as an Interact action while the sparkler is already activated. If you do so, the sparkler can ignite flammable objects the way a [matchstick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3356) does.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sparkler

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bottled Roc](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1947)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 56

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bottle contains a shrunken bird preserved with its feathers intact. When opened, the contents reconstitute into a Gargantuan effigy of a great [roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=352), which can appear in the air instead of on the ground. The roc [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) up to two creatures, then [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to 90 feet and [Releases](/Actions.aspx?ID=83) the creatures. The creatures must be within 15 feet of the roc for it to Grab them; if any of them are unwilling to be [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), the roc must [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) them with a +17 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier or fail to pick them up.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a roc.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Roc

>
[Ghost Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1948)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 56

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A daub of ectoplasm twitches within this glass container, faintly glowing with ghostly light. When opened it forms the echo of a departed spirit, which looks like a Medium [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51). You can throw the ampoule up to 30 feet when you Activate it. The ghost utters a final lament, forcing each living creature in a 15-foot emanation except you to attempt a DC 18 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 3 on a critical failure).

**Craft Requirements** Supply ectoplasmic residue from a destroyed ghost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Ampoule

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hippogriff in a Jar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1949)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 56

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bottle contains a shrunken [hippogriff](/Monsters.aspx?ID=698). When opened, the contents reconstitute into a Large effigy of a hippogriff. The hippogriff waits up to 1 round and allows two creatures to mount it, then Flies up to 65 feet and waits 1 more round to give the mounted creatures time to dismount. Creatures who are still mounted on the hippogriff when it dissolves fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in the space where the hippogriff corpse ends its movement.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a hippogriff.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hippogriff in a Jar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kraken Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1950)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 56

**Price** 5,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Coiled tentacles make it all but impossible to see anything else inside this ampoule. When opened, a Gargantuan [kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=275) bursts forth, which can appear in water instead of on the ground. Its arms attempt to grasp up to four creatures with a reach of 60 feet. The kraken repositions grabbed creatures to a different space within its reach unless the target succeeds at a DC 38 Fortitude save.

 If the kraken is in water, it then releases a cloud of ink in an 80-foot emanation. This cloud has no effect outside of water. Creatures inside the cloud are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), can't use their sense of smell, and are exposed to kraken ink poison. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

**Kraken Ink** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) Krakens are immune to this poison. **Saving Throw** DC 39 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round).

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a kraken.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kraken Bottle

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 56 **Price** 1,300 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- A miniature [mukradi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=303), its chitin shrunken and pale, is coiled within this jar. Its hollow form grows to a Gargantuan shell when you open the jar. It emits one of three breath weapons, chosen by you. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.
  • **Acid Maw** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3)) 10-foot-wide, 60-foot line of acid dealing 12d6 acid damage.
  • **Flame Maw** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 60-foot cone of fire dealing 12d6 fire damage.
  • **Shock Maw** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) 120-foot line of electricity dealing 12d6 electricity damage.
**Craft Requirements** Supply a mukradi corpse.
name: Mukradi Jar
